title: crispy light Russian bun Bao
description: Malty, light Russian dish featuring buns, ideal for brunch. This pescatarian-friendly recipe uses a bento box as a vessel.

ingredients.name: flour
ingredients.notes: unbleached all-purpose flour, such as Bob's Red Mill or King Arthur Flour

ingredients.name: filling
ingredients.notes: pastrami and pickles or cream cheese, chopped scallions, and chopped fresh dill

instructions: Combine warm water and yeast in a small bowl. Stir to dissolve, then let sit 5 minutes until foamy.
Add flour, salt, sugar to a large mixing bowl. Mix together using a stand mixer or by hand until a shaggy dough forms.
Add butter, scalded milk, egg to the dough. Mix until a smooth dough forms, about 5 minutes.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 5-7 minutes until elastic and smooth.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover with a damp cloth. Let rise in a warm place 1 hour, or until doubled in size.
Divide the dough into 6-8 equal pieces. Roll each piece into a ball, then flatten slightly into a disk.
Place a tablespoon of filling in the center of each disk. Fold the dough over the filling, pressing edges together to seal.
Place the buns on a parchment-lined baking sheet, leaving space between each bun. Brush tops with egg wash.
Bake for 18-20 minutes, or until golden brown.
Buns are best served warm, but can be stored in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 days.
